topics.titleTr: RHF ve Zod ile Erişilebilir React Formları
topics.titleEn: Accessible React Forms with RHF and Zod
topics.keywordTr: react hook form zod form doğrulama
topics.angle: how-to + schema-driven validation wired to real accessibility (aria-invalid, role=alert)
topics.brief: Story of rebuilding a broken signup form. Sections: define a Zod schema; wire @hookform/resolvers; mode onBlur for calmer a11y; surface errors with aria-invalid and role=alert; mirror the same schema server-side. Key claims to verify: RHF+Zod is the 2026 default and yields a fully accessible form; onBlur validation is best for accessibility. Primary sources: react-hook-form docs; freeCodeCamp Zod+RHF guide. End with the shared client/server validation pattern and label-association pitfalls.

topics.titleEn: Edge Functions and Rendering: A 2026 Guide
topics.angle: guide + when to run at the edge, cold starts, data locality, and runtime limits
topics.brief: Sections: what edge functions are vs regional serverless; latency wins and their limits; data-locality trap (edge compute far from your DB); streaming SSR at the edge; runtime constraints (no native modules, size caps). Key claims to verify: edge awareness is a core 2026 frontend skill; server-first is the new default. Primary sources: LogRocket 8 trends 2026; talent500 web trends. End with a decision table: render at edge vs origin vs client, with a data-round-trip warning.

topics.titleEn: Are Web Components Ready for 2026?
topics.angle: deep-dive + framework interop, when native custom elements beat a framework
topics.brief: Question-led: can you ship real products on custom elements today? Sections: custom elements, shadow DOM, and slots; SSR/declarative shadow DOM; interop with React/Vue and remaining friction (forms, events, props); design-system reuse across frameworks. Key claims to verify: web components are gaining as a framework-agnostic layer in 2026; declarative shadow DOM closed the SSR gap. Primary sources: Figma 12 web dev trends 2026; MDN web components. End with concrete use-cases where they win and where they still hurt.

topics.titleEn: Signals in Frontend Frameworks, Explained
topics.angle: deep-dive + fine-grained reactivity vs virtual DOM, and what signals change for devs
topics.brief: Framed as: why did every framework suddenly adopt signals? Sections: the reactivity problem; signals vs virtual-DOM diffing; fine-grained updates and fewer re-renders; how Solid, Angular, Preact, and Svelte runes compare; migration and mental-model shifts. Key claims to verify: signals are a defining 2026 DX trend; fine-grained reactivity cuts unnecessary renders. Primary sources: alphonsolabs web trends 2026; framework signals docs. End with a small counter example across two frameworks and when signals are overkill.

topics.titleEn: Idempotent APIs: Safe Retries by Design
topics.angle: how-to + idempotency keys, UPSERT, and dedup so retries never double-charge
topics.brief: Field notes from a double-charge incident. Sections: what idempotency means for POST; idempotency keys and dedup tables; UPSERT (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) vs failing inserts; conditional updates with preconditions; TTL and key scoping. Key claims to verify: 2026 interviews stress idempotent, concurrency-safe APIs; UPSERT is the recommended retry-safe pattern. Primary sources: DigitalApplied API rate-limiting reference; Devinterview concurrency questions. End with a request-flow diagram and a test that fires the same request twice.

topics.titleTr: Rate Limiting: Token Bucket ve Sliding Window
topics.titleEn: Rate Limiting: Token Bucket vs Sliding Window
topics.angle: comparison + algorithms plus the three design decisions (where, unit, algorithm)
topics.brief: Sections: the three decisions (edge vs service, per-key vs per-IP vs per-route, which algorithm); fixed window, sliding window log, sliding window counter, token bucket, leaky bucket; memory vs accuracy tradeoffs. Key claims to verify: sliding window counter is O(1) memory, near-exact, with Cloudflare production use; limiter placement changes behavior. Primary sources: DigitalApplied rate-limiting 2026 reference; Cloudflare engineering. End with a comparison table and pseudo-code for token bucket and sliding window counter.

topics.titleEn: Retries, Backoff, and Circuit Breakers
topics.angle: guide + resilient client patterns that fail fast without retry storms
topics.brief: Field notes on a retry storm that took down a dependency. Sections: when to retry vs fail; exponential backoff with jitter; retry budgets and caps; circuit breaker states (closed/open/half-open); pairing with idempotency and timeouts. Key claims to verify: safe retries need idempotency + backoff + jitter + limits + breakers; retries without idempotency cause duplicate side effects. Primary sources: Devinterview concurrency questions; AWS builder library on timeouts/retries. End with default parameter values and an anti-pattern list.

topics.titleEn: How to Fix the N+1 Query Problem
topics.angle: how-to + detection, eager loading, and batching to kill hidden query fan-out
topics.brief: Story of a list endpoint that fired 400 queries. Sections: what N+1 is and why ORMs cause it; detecting it (query logs, APM, assertions in tests); fixes (eager loading/JOIN, dataloader batching, projection); when denormalization or caching is warranted. Key claims to verify: N+1 is a top ORM performance mistake; batching collapses N queries to one. Primary sources: ORM docs (Prisma/Hibernate) on eager loading; database performance references. End with a before/after query count and a regression test that asserts a query budget.

topics.titleEn: Feature Flags Without the Tech Debt
topics.angle: guide + flag lifecycle, cleanup discipline, and kill switches that don't rot
topics.brief: Contrarian: most flag systems become permanent debt. Sections: flag types (release, ops/kill switch, experiment, permission); short-lived vs long-lived; naming, ownership, and expiry dates; cleanup workflow and stale-flag detection; testing both branches. Key claims to verify: unmanaged flags create combinatorial test debt; kill switches decouple deploy from release. Primary sources: Martin Fowler feature-toggles article; OpenFeature docs. End with a lifecycle checklist and a policy that every flag needs a removal date and owner.

topics.titleEn: Zero-Downtime Database Schema Migrations
topics.angle: how-to + expand-contract pattern, safe backfills, and dual-write windows
topics.brief: Story of an ALTER that locked a table in production. Sections: expand-contract (add nullable, backfill, dual-write, switch reads, drop old); avoiding long locks; batched backfills; adding indexes concurrently; coordinating app and schema rollout. Key claims to verify: expand-contract enables zero-downtime column changes; CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY avoids write locks in Postgres. Primary sources: Postgres docs on locking/concurrent indexes; expand-contract pattern references. End with a step ordering that lets you roll back at any stage.

topics.titleEn: Getting Started with OpenTelemetry
topics.angle: how-to + instrument a service end-to-end and export traces, metrics, and logs vendor-neutrally
topics.brief: Walkthrough of instrumenting one service. Sections: SDK vs auto-instrumentation; the Collector as a routing hub; exporting to an open-source backend to dodge lock-in; sampling and cardinality control. Key claims to verify: OTel + self-hosted backends is the 2026 standard for neutralizing vendor lock-in; centralized OTel can cut MTTR up to 70% and improve DORA metrics. Primary sources: HAMS Tech OpenTelemetry-on-Kubernetes 2026; OpenTelemetry docs. End with a minimal Collector config and a first-trace validation step.

topics.titleEn: eBPF for Observability, Explained
topics.angle: deep-dive + zero-instrumentation monitoring and the modern eBPF stack
topics.brief: Question-led: how do you get traces without touching app code? Sections: what eBPF is and why the kernel gives free visibility; the 2026 stack (Cilium+Hubble for network, Pixie for APM, Tetragon for security, Beyla for OTel spans); limits and overhead. Key claims to verify: AWS EKS defaulted to Cilium; eBPF data path is 30-40% higher throughput than iptables; eBPF mainstreamed in 2026. Primary sources: DEV eBPF 2026 kernel revolution; Gheware eBPF Kubernetes observability. End with when eBPF complements vs replaces SDK instrumentation.

topics.titleEn: Kubernetes Autoscaling: HPA, VPA, and CA
topics.angle: guide + combining HPA, VPA, and Cluster Autoscaler without them fighting each other
topics.brief: Sections: HPA (horizontal, by metric), VPA (right-sizing requests), Cluster Autoscaler (node scaling), and why HPA+VPA on the same resource/metric conflict; custom and external metrics; resource quotas for fairness; cost impact. Key claims to verify: 86% of cloud environments lack HPA, quietly inflating spend; combining the three is the recommended data-driven approach. Primary sources: KodeKloud Kubernetes best practices; ScaleOps/Spot cost-optimization 2026. End with a config matrix and the HPA/VPA conflict workaround.

topics.titleEn: Secrets Management in the Cloud, Done Right
topics.angle: how-to + moving off .env files to managed secrets with rotation and least privilege
topics.brief: Contrarian: your .env file is a breach waiting to happen. Sections: why env files and committed secrets fail; managed options (cloud secret managers, Vault, SOPS-encrypted git); short-lived dynamic credentials; automatic rotation; scoping and audit. Key claims to verify: dynamic short-lived credentials beat long-lived keys; SOPS lets you keep encrypted secrets in git safely. Primary sources: HashiCorp Vault docs; Mozilla SOPS. End with a migration path from .env to a secret manager and a rotation checklist. No real secrets in examples.

topics.titleEn: Blue-Green vs Canary Deployments
topics.angle: comparison + rollout strategies, rollback speed, and cost tradeoffs
topics.brief: Question-led: which rollout fits your risk tolerance? Sections: blue-green (two full environments, instant switch, instant rollback, double cost); canary (gradual traffic shift, metric gates, cheaper, slower); rolling as the baseline; database-change coupling and feature-flag pairing. Key claims to verify: blue-green gives fastest rollback but doubles infra; canary limits blast radius via progressive traffic. Primary sources: Google/GitLab deployment-strategy docs; CNCF progressive-delivery (Argo Rollouts/Flagger). End with a decision table by team maturity and workload.

topics.titleEn: Postgres Connection Pooling with PgBouncer
topics.angle: how-to + pool modes, sizing, and the RLS/session footguns behind a pooler
topics.brief: Contrarian: pooling silently breaks your tenant isolation. Sections: why Postgres connections are expensive; PgBouncer multiplexing (e.g. 500 app to 20 DB connections); pool modes (session/transaction/statement) and what each disables; the RLS footgun (shared role makes current_user useless behind a pooler); fix with SET LOCAL in a transaction. Key claims to verify: transaction pooling breaks session features; SET LOCAL resets tenant context safely. Primary sources: Bytebase Postgres RLS footguns; PgBouncer docs. Note PgBouncer 1.25.2 CVE-2026-6664 patch. End with sizing guidance.

topics.titleEn: The Staff Engineer Path: Beyond Senior
topics.angle: guide + scope, influence, and the writing habit that gets you promoted
topics.brief: Story of a senior who plateaued until they started writing. Sections: what Staff means (technical leadership without reports, cross-team multiplier); archetypes (tech lead, architect, solver, right hand); how to demonstrate scope; sponsorship vs mentorship; the RFC/design-doc habit as highest-leverage signal. Key claims to verify: writing design docs and RFCs is the single highest-leverage skill for Staff promotion; Staff total comp ~$400K-$800K at top firms. Primary sources: jobsbyculture Staff Engineer 2026; StaffEng.com. End with a 90-day plan to build a promotion case.

topics.titleEn: How to Write an Engineering RFC
topics.angle: how-to + a reusable template, tradeoff framing, and a review flow that converges
topics.brief: Field notes on RFCs that shipped vs stalled. Sections: when an RFC is worth it; a template (context, goals/non-goals, options with tradeoffs, decision, risks, rollout); writing for skimmers; running async review and closing debate; recording the decision. Key claims to verify: clear written technical communication is the top Staff-track skill; documented tradeoffs speed alignment. Primary sources: jobsbyculture Staff Engineer 2026; Oxide/Rust RFC process. End with a fill-in template and anti-patterns (solution-first, no non-goals, unbounded scope).

topics.titleEn: Salary Negotiation for Developers in 2026
topics.angle: guide + using impact metrics and comp bands as leverage
topics.brief: Sections: researching bands (levels data, transparency laws); why you let them name a range first; building an impact dossier (latency, error rate, cost per request, revenue); the AI-skills wage premium; competing offers and total-comp components. Key claims to verify: AI-skilled workers earn a 56% wage premium (PwC 2025, up from 25%); documented production metrics strengthen negotiation; ask for the range instead of stating a number. Primary sources: HackerX tech salary guide 2026; PwC Global AI Jobs Barometer. End with a metrics template and scripts.

topics.titleEn: Building an AI-Proof Developer Skill Set
topics.angle: contrarian + what stays valuable when AI writes most of the code
topics.brief: Contrarian: chasing the newest framework is a losing bet; judgment isn't. Sections: skills that compound (system design, debugging, reviewing AI output, writing, product sense); the risk of over- or under-using AI; proving impact with metrics. Key claims to verify: recruiters value architect-level thinking and clear communication over framework mastery; the productive middle path is thoughtful AI integration, not rejection or blind acceptance. Primary sources: Rootstack tech skills 2026; zenvanriel AI career pathways 2026. End with a self-audit and a learning plan weighted to durable skills.

topics.titleEn: How to Run Effective Code Reviews
topics.angle: guide + scope, tone, speed, and reviewing AI-generated pull requests
topics.brief: Contrarian: nitpicking style wastes everyone's time; review for risk. Sections: what review is for (correctness, design, maintainability, not formatting a linter handles); small PRs and fast turnaround; tone and blocking vs non-blocking comments; author responsibilities; reviewing AI-generated PRs skeptically. Key claims to verify: fast, focused reviews reduce cycle time; AI-written PRs need extra scrutiny as AI slop rises. Primary sources: Google engineering code-review guidelines; SmartBear/industry review data. End with a reviewer checklist and a comment-severity convention.

topics.titleEn: Building in Public: A Developer's Playbook
topics.angle: guide + audience, cadence, and what to share without oversharing
topics.brief: Question-led: does building in public actually help a dev career? Sections: why it compounds (network, hiring signal, feedback); what to share (progress, learnings, metrics) vs what to keep private (secrets, unshipped strategy); cadence and platform choice; turning a build log into a portfolio. Key claims to verify: visible work and clear communication are hiring signals in 2026; documenting impact aids promotion and negotiation. Primary sources: Rootstack tech skills 2026; indie-hacker build-in-public case studies. End with a lightweight weekly-sharing routine.
